messages.content:
Two-point arbitrage is also known as ________.  
A) geographic arbitrage B) discount arbitrage C) forward arbitrage  D) equilibrium arbitrage

Famous Footwear imports a variety of athletic shoes, work boots, and casual footwear from around the world. Which of the following would most likely provide Famous Footwear with a detailed classification scheme for its imported shoes?
A) tariff rate quota      B) harmonized tariff schedule  C) compound tariff report D) ad valorem report

Country X imposes a low tariff rate for the first 5,000 pounds of rice it imports from Japan and a high tariff rate on all imported rice above this threshold. Country X most likely uses a ________. 
A) tariff rate quota      B) free trade zone  C) voluntary export restraint D) foreign currency control

France's requirement that during prime time at least 40 percent of the songs played on 1700 AM and FM stations must be written or sung by French or Francophone artists is an example of which type of nontariff barrier?         
A) local-purchase requirements
B) numerical export controls
C) product and testing standards
D) public-sector procurement policies
E) restricted access to distribution networks
E) restricted access to distribution networks

Toyota, ExxonMobil, General Electric, and Volkswagen are all examples of ________.           
A) management contracts B) multinational corporations C) portfolio investments   D) franchised firms

Hawthorne Enterprises, an Australian firm, has plans to open a manufacturing facility in France next year. Some executives at the firm are unhappy with this decision, most likely because France ________.
There is not enough information provided to determine why some executives at Hawthorne Enterprises may be unhappy with the decision to open a manufacturing facility in France.

Country X has 14 official languages and nearly 2,000 dialects. Which of the following is most likely a true statement?      
 
A) Nonverbal communication is unlikely to be very effective in Country X.
B) Nationhood is the defining factor of the society in Country X.
C) Income and value differences exist among the citizens of Country X.
D) MNEs should avoid Country X due to costly adaptation needs.
C) Income and value differences exist among the citizens of Country X.

In a ________ culture, a speaker's words explicitly convey the speaker's message.         
A) linguistic    B) high-context           C) verbal         D) low-context

What is the first step in acculturation?    
A) self-reference criterion       B) language training C) cross-cultural literacy D) cultural conversion
